mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or flat after a prior tightening, lower policy rates reduce discount rates and financial stress, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not re-accelerating, because disinflation preserves the easing impulse.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods where FEDFUNDS declines on a 3-month basis and CPIAUCSL is below its 6-month trend, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which raises recession probability and depresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 60-day forward SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the median forward return is nonnegative across a 3-year rolling test window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger labor income and retail demand, supporting earnings expectations and pushing SPX higher over the next month. The effect should be absent when either series weakens on a 3-month basis.
failure_condition: Reject if joint-strength periods in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not outperform baseline SPX returns over 20 trading days, or if either proxy alone explains the same effect with no interaction gain.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and inventory pressure, which usually precedes profit downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative monthly changes in INDPRO fail to correspond to lower 20-day forward SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not below 50% over any out-of-sample rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST from its recent level signals a sharp housing-demand downturn that feeds into construction employment, household wealth, and cyclicals, producing negative SPX returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing contractions do not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears once the threshold is applied out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, real rates are restrictive and equity multiples compress, so SPX should underperform over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes with elevated CPIAUCSL do not show negative forward 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are positive in most triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS while UNRATE is stable or falling indicates labor-market resilience, supporting consumption and earnings, which should lift SPX over the next week. The signal fails when employment gains are offset by rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if weeks with rising PAYEMS and nonrising UNRATE do not produce positive 5-day forward SPX returns more than 55% of the time over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive revision-free growth pulse in GDPC1 together with rising RSAFS indicates broad final demand, which should support equities over the next quarter through better earnings expectations and lower recession odds.
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized strength in GDP and retail sales does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns out of sample, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value versus either proxy alone.
